summary:
Alpha Capital scored 43/100 in our full review. The firm offers four evaluation models — Alpha One (1-step), Alpha Pro (2-step with 10%, 8%, and 6% variants), Swing (2-step), and Alpha Three (3-step) — with platform access via MT5, cTrader, DXTrade, and TradeLocker.

Profit splits are 80% across all models. Payout schedules depend on the plan: Swing and Alpha One support on-demand payouts, while Alpha Pro and Alpha Three use bi-weekly payouts. On-demand payouts require compliance with the 40% Best Day Rule and at least 2% gross profit. Bi-weekly accounts need minimum $100 profit and 5 trading days for the first withdrawal.

Drawdown and daily loss rules are model-specific. Alpha One uses 4% daily and 6% trailing max drawdown. Alpha Pro comes in three versions: Pro 10% (5% daily, 10% static), Pro 8% (4% daily, 8% static), and Pro 6% (3% daily, 6% static). Swing uses 5% daily and 10% static. Alpha Three uses 4% daily and 6% static. News trading is allowed during assessment but restricted on qualified accounts with plan-specific windows. Weekend holding is allowed on Swing, One, and Three qualified accounts but not on Pro.

Alpha Capital also has a live progression route called Alpha Prime, where traders can qualify by achieving $40,000 profit on one simulated payout, five payouts from one qualified account, or $100,000 in lifetime payouts. The firm offers Standard and Raw assessment types — Standard has no commission while Raw charges $2.5 per lot each direction, with indices commission-free on both. US residents can use cTrader, DXTrade, and TradeLocker but cannot use MT5. Maximum allocation is $400,000 per household with $300,000 per strategy on qualified accounts.
pros: Four evaluation models (Alpha One, Alpha Pro with 3 variants, Swing, Alpha Three) covering different trading styles
Account sizes up to $200K with scaling plan up to $2 million
MT5, cTrader, DXTrade, and TradeLocker platform options
Alpha Prime live progression route for top performers
Standard and Raw assessment types with commission-free indices
Autochartist integration, academy section, blog content, and market analysis tools
0.25% performance fee bonus on fourth successful payout
cons: No real-money trading — fully simulated environment until Alpha Prime
No transparency on average spreads per instrument
Reports of payout denials and sudden account closures
Strict KYC enforcement after passing challenges
Rules vary significantly between plans — drawdown, news trading, weekend holding, and payout schedules are all plan-specific
40% Best Day Rule limits daily profit contribution on on-demand accounts
bestFor: Traders who want multiple evaluation paths with plan-specific flexibility, Standard or Raw pricing options, and a stated live progression route through Alpha Prime
notIdealFor: Traders seeking consistent rules across all account types, transparent spread data, or those uncomfortable with a simulated environment where reports of payout denials and strict post-challenge KYC enforcement persist

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On Alpha Capital
verdictBlocks.body:
Alpha Capital scored 43/100 overall. The firm offers four evaluation models — Alpha One (1-step), Alpha Pro (2-step in 10%, 8%, and 6% variants), Swing (2-step), and Alpha Three (3-step) — with competitive challenge fees from $50 and platform access across MT5, cTrader, DXTrade, and TradeLocker.

Drawdown rules are plan-specific rather than firm-wide. Alpha One uses 4% daily and 6% trailing max drawdown. Alpha Pro comes in three versions: Pro 10% (5% daily, 10% static), Pro 8% (4% daily, 8% static), and Pro 6% (3% daily, 6% static). Swing uses 5% daily and 10% static. Alpha Three uses 8%/4%/4% targets with 4% daily and 6% static max drawdown. News trading is allowed during assessment but restricted on qualified accounts — Pro 8%/10% uses a 2-minute window, Pro 6%, One, and Three use 5 minutes, and Swing allows news trading but trades in the 4-minute window must last more than 2 minutes. Weekend holding is allowed on Swing, One, and Three qualified accounts but not on Pro.

Payout schedules depend on the plan: Swing and Alpha One support on-demand payouts (requiring 40% Best Day Rule compliance and 2% gross profit), while Alpha Pro and Alpha Three use bi-weekly payouts (minimum $100 profit and 5 trading days for first withdrawal). On the fourth successful payout, Alpha adds a 0.25% performance fee bonus of 0.25% of initial account size.

Alpha Capital now offers Standard and Raw assessment types — Standard has no commission while Raw charges $2.5 per lot each direction, with indices commission-free on both. The firm also has a live progression route called Alpha Prime, where traders qualify through $40,000 profit on one simulated payout, five payouts from one qualified account, or $100,000 lifetime payouts.

Maximum allocation is $400,000 per household with $300,000 per strategy on qualified accounts. Only qualified accounts can be merged (same type, same platform, up to $300K, no scaling after merge). Additional rules include a 2-minute average trade duration requirement, 30-day inactivity limit, and hedging allowed within the same account but not across accounts.

The main concerns remain: all trading takes place in a simulated environment, reports of payout denials and sudden account closures persist, and strict KYC enforcement after challenges has drawn criticism. For traders who can navigate plan-specific rules and are comfortable with the simulated model, Alpha Capital offers genuine flexibility. But traders should closely review their chosen plan's terms before committing.

faq.a: Alpha Capital Group is a proprietary trading firm that offers simulated funded accounts rather than real-money trading. It provides four evaluation models (Alpha One, Alpha Pro, Swing, Alpha Three) across MT5, cTrader, DXTrade, and TradeLocker. The firm has a stated live progression route through Alpha Prime. However, concerns over payout denials, sudden account closures, and strict KYC enforcement have been reported. Traders should approach with caution and carefully review the terms before joining.

Alpha Capital offers four evaluation models with plan-specific rules. Alpha One is a 1-step evaluation. Alpha Pro is a 2-step with three variants (10%, 8%, 6%) offering different targets and drawdowns. Swing is a 2-step for overnight and weekend holding. Alpha Three is a 3-step. Traders pass assessments to qualify for funded accounts (simulated). Standard assessments have no commission, while Raw assessments charge $2.5 per lot each direction. Payout options are on-demand (Swing, One) or bi-weekly (Pro, Three). Top performers can progress to Alpha Prime for live capital.

faq.q: Is Alpha Capital Group accepting US clients?
faq.a: Yes, Alpha Capital accepts US clients. US residents can use cTrader, DXTrade, and TradeLocker — they are not restricted to DXTrade only. The only restriction is that US residents cannot use MT5. Traders traveling to the US also cannot access their MT5 accounts while within the country.

faq.q: What broker does Alpha Capital use?
faq.a: Alpha Capital Group is broker-backed through ACG Markets, an FSA-regulated brokerage. While ACG Markets provides Prime-of-Prime liquidity and ultra-low latency execution, all trading within Alpha Capital remains in a simulated environment until a trader qualifies for Alpha Prime, the firm's live progression route.

Alpha Prime is Alpha Capital's stated live progression route where traders trade with real capital. Traders can qualify by achieving $40,000 profit on one simulated payout, five payouts from one qualified account, or $100,000 in lifetime payouts. This provides a clear path from simulated to live trading, though traders should verify current qualification criteria directly with the firm.

flaggedWarning:
Alpha Capital operates in a **simulated environment** with no real-money execution until traders qualify for **Alpha Prime** (the firm's live progression route). Reports of **payout denials, sudden account closures, and strict KYC enforcement** after passing challenges persist. Rules — including drawdown, news trading, weekend holding, and payout schedules — **vary significantly between plans**.

Our testing scored Alpha Capital 43/100, and we only recommend firms scoring 50/100+. For better trust and pricing, consider [IC Funded](/reviews/ic-funded), [Blueberry Funded](/reviews/blueberry-funded), or [BrightFunded](/reviews/brightfunded) for more reliable prop firms.
